title: Sour Tamarind Moroccan Sea Bass Tagine
description: A bright and sophisticated tagine that balances the sour intensity of tamarind with the delicate sweetness of sea bass and traditional Moroccan aromatics.

ingredients.name: Tamarind pulp ball
ingredients.notes: Seedless, softened in 1/2 cup warm water

instructions: Strain the softened tamarind through a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the solids and keeping the concentrated liquid paste.
In a small bowl, whisk together the tamarind paste, ginger, garlic, cumin, saffron, and half of the cilantro to create a chermoula marinade.
Coat the sea bass fillets with half of the marinade and let sit for 15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a tagine or heavy-bottomed skillet over medium heat and sear the sea bass skin-side down for 3 minutes until crisp.
Remove the fish temporarily and set aside; add the cherry tomatoes to the pan and sauté until they begin to burst.
Deglaze the pan with the white fish stock, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Whisk in the remaining marinade and the diced preserved lemon rind to create the poaching liquid.
Return the fish to the pan, skin-side up, cover with the tagine lid, and simmer on low for 6-8 minutes until the fish is opaque and flakes easily.
Garnish with the remaining fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
